message.content: Adversaries may gather victim identity information to enhance their targeting efforts. This information can include personal data such as employee names and email addresses, as well as sensitive details like credentials. Common methods of gathering this information include phishing, active scanning, and exploiting publicly accessible data sets such as social media or victim-owned websites. This information can facilitate further reconnaissance, establish operational resources, and enable initial access through techniques like phishing or using valid accounts. Notable groups such as APT32, HEXANE, LAPSUS$, and Magic Hound have employed these tactics to target specific individuals based on their roles and privileges. Mitigation strategies include implementing strong authentication mechanisms, conducting regular security awareness training, monitoring for unusual account activity, and limiting the exposure of sensitive information online.
